claims.statement: ADR-0004 and SPEC-0001 require separate revocable device credentials restricted to one Show and explicit action scopes.
ADR-0008 requires credential audience and exact feedback authority to be established outside the signed feedback envelope.
The current server persists only one global Output token digest and has no device principal, device token record, or device scope resolver.

H-005: Given externally authenticated Owner authority and trusted clock, entropy, secret codec, and store adapters, an opaque one-time bearer secret backed by a sealed record binding credential identity, generation, Show, targets, controls, explicit scopes, expiration, and revocation can establish a device principal independent of Studio identity, while fresh credential resolution makes rotation, expiration, and revocation fail closed before action authorization or feedback admission.

Review accepted H-005 only for the adapter-neutral lifecycle under externally established Owner, clock, entropy, secret-codec, and store authority after role, sealed-secret, identity-separation, scope, target, control, tampering, rotation, generation-audience, expiration, revocation, and feedback-admission tests passed.

This Slice does not determine Owner session binding, production clock or entropy sources, the production secret codec, durable store, HTTP or WebSocket transport, rate limiting, concurrent in-flight revocation semantics, complete action catalog, server feedback signing-key lifecycle, sequence persistence, Companion configuration, or hardware integration.

successCriteria.statement: The lifecycle requires an externally established Owner principal to issue, rotate, or revoke a credential, and the returned bearer token is exposed once while the store receives only a sealed secret.
successCriteria.verification: Role-bound lifecycle, one-time token, and sealed-storage tests.

successCriteria.statement: Authentication resolves an active token to a device-only authority containing its immutable credential identity, generation, Show, targets, controls, scopes, and expiration without Studio roles or session identity.
Authorization rejects another Show, undeclared scope, unauthorized target, and unauthorized control before a domain action can execute.
Rotation preserves credential identity, increments generation, changes the feedback audience, and invalidates the predecessor; expiration and revocation reject authentication and further rotation.
successCriteria.verification: Bearer and feedback-audience lifecycle invalidation tests.

successCriteria.statement: Fresh authentication of a credential projects the exact external authority required by ADR-0008, expired or revoked credentials cannot produce that context, and an envelope issued for another credential audience is rejected.
successCriteria.verification: Signed feedback composition and audience-substitution tests.

successCriteria.statement: The Slice proves only adapter-neutral credential lifecycle and authority resolution and makes no persistence, endpoint, network, or hardware claim.

definitionOfDone.statement: CHG-0014 records SDD-005 as an accepted security experiment and separates facts, H-005, the reviewed inference, and remaining unknowns.
The Slice adds only an adapter-neutral lifecycle, in-memory test store, authority projection, and focused tests; no route, socket, file store, UI, or hardware adapter is added.
Review attempts to refute H-005 through non-Owner issuance, malformed grants, Show, scope, target, control, predecessor, expiration, revocation, and audience substitution.
ADR-0009 was created only after human review accepted H-005 within the demonstrated adapter-neutral boundary.
definitionOfDone.evidence: ADR-0009 context and Human Doctrine review

definitionOfDone.statement: Governance, focused protocol quality, and the full repository checks and build pass for the final Slice manifest.
definitionOfDone.evidence: npm run governance:check, protocol checks, npm run check, and npm run build
